proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (age_years) is positively correlated with longer pfs_months.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have shorter mean pfs_months than males (sex_female=0).
Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Stage-IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV.
Brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Mean pfs_months differs across smoking_status groups (never/former/current).
Adenocarcinoma histology has longer pfs_months than squamous histology.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=0.757, p=<1e-300; older patients have longer PFS.
Mean PFS: female 3.32 vs male 3.52 (diff -0.20 mo, p=7.70e-25).
OLS slope = -1.099 mo per unit ECOG, p=<1e-300.
Stage IV mean 2.92 vs non-IV 4.36 (diff -1.44 mo, p=<1e-300).
Diff (brain mets vs none) = -0.92 mo, p=<1e-300.
One-way ANOVA F=433.0, p=3.65e-187. Means: never=3.67, former=3.61, current=2.99. Current<former~never; current minus never = -0.69 mo.

analyses.code: Welch t-test PFS adeno vs squamous
analyses.result_summary: adeno 3.67 vs squamous 2.81 (diff +0.86 mo, p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: EGFR-mutant patients (egfr_mutation=1) have longer pfs_months than egfr_mutation=0 patients.
KRAS G12C-mutant patients (kras_g12c=1) have longer pfs_months than kras_g12c=0 patients.
ALK-fusion-positive patients (alk_fusion=1) have shorter pfs_months than alk_fusion=0 patients.
STK11-mutant patients (stk11_mutation=1) have different (any direction) pfs_months than stk11_mutation=0 patients.
BRCA2-mutant patients (brca2_mutation=1) have different (any direction) pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0 patients.
TMB-high patients (tmb_high=1) have different (any direction) pfs_months than tmb_high=0 patients.
Higher pdl1_tps is correlated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: egfr_mutation=1 mean 3.60 vs egfr_mutation=0 mean 3.41 (diff +0.19 mo, p=6.48e-11).
kras_g12c=1 mean 4.11 vs kras_g12c=0 mean 3.33 (diff +0.78 mo, p=7.36e-94).
alk_fusion=1 mean 2.66 vs alk_fusion=0 mean 3.47 (diff -0.81 mo, p=5.61e-66).
stk11_mutation=1 mean 3.44 vs stk11_mutation=0 mean 3.43 (diff +0.01 mo, p=7.41e-01).
brca2_mutation=1 mean 3.32 vs brca2_mutation=0 mean 3.44 (diff -0.12 mo, p=3.12e-02).
tmb_high=1 mean 3.30 vs tmb_high=0 mean 3.49 (diff -0.19 mo, p=1.04e-17).
Pearson r=0.0063, p=1.57e-01: no detectable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: albumin_g_dl is positively correlated with pfs_months.
ldh_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
weight_loss_pct_6mo is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
crp_mg_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
nlr is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
hemoglobin_g_dl is positively correlated with pfs_months.
alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
ast_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
alt_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
total_bilirubin_mg_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
creatinine_mg_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
bun_mg_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
sodium_meq_l is positively correlated with pfs_months.
potassium_meq_l is negatively (deviation from norm) correlated with pfs_months.
calcium_mg_dl is negatively (hypercalcemia) correlated with pfs_months.
treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 (unadjusted, marginal effect).
treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 (unadjusted, marginal effect).
treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 (unadjusted, marginal effect).
treatment_osimertinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0 (unadjusted, marginal effect).

analyses.result_summary: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 mean 3.41 vs =0 mean 3.45 (diff -0.035 mo, p=8.11e-02).
treatment_sotorasib=1 mean 3.64 vs =0 mean 3.32 (diff +0.322 mo, p=5.62e-49).
treatment_olaparib=1 mean 3.41 vs =0 mean 3.44 (diff -0.025 mo, p=2.56e-01).
treatment_osimertinib=1 mean 3.43 vs =0 mean 3.43 (diff +0.004 mo, p=8.70e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for demographics, ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, all biomarkers and labs, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 (positive adjusted main effect).

After adjusting for demographics, ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, all biomarkers and labs, treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 (positive adjusted main effect).

After adjusting for demographics, ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, all biomarkers and labs, treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 (positive adjusted main effect).

After adjusting for demographics, ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, all biomarkers and labs, treatment_osimertinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0 (positive adjusted main effect).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_sotorasib × (kras_g12c=1) interaction on pfs_months is positive: sotorasib lengthens PFS more in KRAS G12C+ patients than in KRAS-WT.
treatment_sotorasib × (kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0) joint subgroup interaction is positive: the sotorasib benefit is concentrated specifically in KRAS G12C+ males.
Within KRAS G12C+ males (kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0), the unadjusted sotorasib effect is larger than the unadjusted effect in KRAS G12C+ overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the strict subgroup defined by kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND alk_fusion=0, treatment_sotorasib=1 yields substantially longer pfs_months than =0 (positive effect ≥4 months).
In the complement of the strict subgroup (NOT [kras_g12c=1 & sex_female=0 & brca2_mutation=0 & alk_fusion=0]), treatment_sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months (null effect outside subgroup).
treatment_sotorasib × strict_grp interaction on pfs_months is positive and very large (≈+4-5 months), and the residual main treatment_sotorasib effect outside the strict group is null.

analyses.code: Welch t-test + adjusted OLS within strict_grp==1
analyses.result_summary: n=3266 (n_tx=1154); unadjusted diff +4.99 mo, p=<1e-300; adjusted β = +4.93, p=<1e-300.
Interaction β = +4.93, p=<1e-300; tx main β = +0.0012 (p=6.94e-01). For comparison, kras+ & male alone: diff = +4.64 mo (p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all 1- and 2-feature binary subgroups, there exists a subgroup in which treatment_pembrolizumab=1 yields a substantial PFS benefit (>0.3 mo) over treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
Across all 1- and 2-feature binary subgroups, there exists a subgroup in which treatment_olaparib=1 yields a substantial PFS benefit (>0.3 mo) over treatment_olaparib=0.
Across all 1- and 2-feature binary subgroups, there exists a subgroup in which treatment_osimertinib=1 yields a substantial PFS benefit (>0.3 mo) over treatment_osimertinib=0.

analyses.result_summary: Best subgroup for treatment_pembrolizumab: 'stage_iv=0 & kras_g12c=1' (n=2265), unadjusted diff -0.458 mo, p=1.54e-04. All sub-rule effects |diff|<0.5 mo and most are non-significant after multiple testing — no clinically meaningful subgroup found.

Best subgroup for treatment_olaparib: 'stage_iv=1 & hist_adeno=0' (n=9075), unadjusted diff -0.139 mo, p=2.14e-03. All sub-rule effects |diff|<0.5 mo and most are non-significant after multiple testing — no clinically meaningful subgroup found.

Best subgroup for treatment_osimertinib: 'brca2_mutation=1 & tmb_high=0' (n=1084), unadjusted diff -0.404 mo, p=3.77e-03. All sub-rule effects |diff|<0.5 mo and most are non-significant after multiple testing — no clinically meaningful subgroup found.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0, an OLS adjusted for ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, all biomarkers, all labs, and all other treatments shows treatment_sotorasib=1 yields longer pfs_months than =0 (robust, large adjusted effect).
Within kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=1 (KRAS G12C+ females), treatment_sotorasib has no detectable effect on pfs_months (null effect adjusted).
